creator: bardou.p@mipih.fr
text:
I suppose I don't need to load the agp module as it is built in my
kernel (I use the GENERIC STABLE amd64 whith no personnal modifications,
there's "device agp" in it).

If that may help, I tried to put the same HDD into another computer with
a 945G chipset. After a little "Xorg -configure" I succeed to run X with
"Xorg -config /root/xorg.conf.new". I also run linux on the same box
(the one with the G33), and xorg works perfectly. So I really think the
problem is the IGP kernel driver.

Full pciconf -lv :
stage003:~ # pciconf -lv
